original_abstract: This document presents the updated clinical practice guidelines and recommendations issued by the Polish Society of Hypertension. Developed by a multidisciplinary panel of leading experts, including specialists in hypertension, cardiology, nephrology, and primary care, these recommendations provide a comprehensive framework for the diagnosis, evaluation, and management of arterial hypertension. The guidelines integrate the latest clinical trial evidence and international consensus to optimize patient outcomes, addressing key areas such as pharmacological strategies, lifestyle interventions, and the management of hypertension-related comorbidities within the specific context of the Polish healthcare system.
humanized_abstract: The updated Clinical Practice Guidelines for Arterial Hypertension with Recommendations were prepared by the Polish Society of Hypertension. An interdisciplinary group of Polish hypertension, cardiology, nephrology and primary care experts made the recommendations. The updated guidelines translate the most up-to-date evidence derived from clinical trials as well as an international consensu s and provide effective clinical tools in the management of arterial hypertension, addressing therapeutic strategies, non-pharmacological measurements and treatment of co-morbid conditions, all tailored to the situation in the Polish health care system.
